title: allium achaium subsp. parnassicum
intro: Allium achaium subsp. parnassicum, also known as Parnassian garlic or mountain garlic, is a rare and endemic species of the Allium genus, characterized by its bulbous shape and strong onion-garlic scent. Traditionally, Allium achaium subsp. parnassicum has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of consuming Allium achaium subsp. parnassicum include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Allium achaium subsp. parnassicum can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
